english: I. Cs. 'clean', Sch. also 'clear, careful'; bzáb-pa (bzab pa) Cs. 'fine, elegant'. In books I met with neither form; in col. language, however, are used: *záb-mo* (*zab mo*) 1. dressed up, smart, = mčór-po (mchor po). 2. fond of dress, vain. — *zab-če* (*zab che*) W. to dress one's self up. — *záb-gos* (*zab gos*) W. festival raiment, holiday-clothes (opp. to rgyún-gos (rgyun gos)). — *zab-tód* (*zab thod*) W., *zab-tó* (*zab tho*) C. (lit.: sprod (sprod)) *taṅ soṅ* (*thang song*) he is dressed up, very smart. — Sch.: γzáb-yig (gzab yig), 'elegant writing', the Tibetan printed letters, dbú-čan (dbu can). — II. v. γzábs-pa (gzabs pa).

, also γzáb-pa (gzab pa), γzágs-pa (gzags pa) Lex., imp. γzobs (gzobs), to use care, diligence, lo γčig (lo gcig) zas-spyód (zas spyod) γzábs-pas (gzabs pas) by a careful diet continued for a year Maṅ.; to take care, to beware, dé-las (de las) γzobs (gzobs) beware of it, be on your guard against it Sch.
Lex., peg, hook, wooden nail, for hanging up things; γzar-sláṅ (gzar slang) a pan that may be hung up.

(col. zá-ru (za ru)) ladle, gen. of wood, γzár-bu (gzar bu) ₒpyar ('phyar) she wields the ladle, she swings it for a blow Mil.; dgáṅ-γzar (dgang gzar) and blúgs-γzar (blugs gzar) two spoons or ladles, with long handles, used at burnt-offerings Schl. 249.

1. adj. γzár-po (gzar po), steep, rugged, precipitous, brag mtó-la (brag mtho la) γzár-ba-la (gzar ba la) near a high, precipitous rock Mil.; ri (ri) γzár-po (gzar po), brag γzár-po (brag gzar po) slope, declivity, of a hill or rock; brag-yɣaṅ-γzár (brag g.yang gzar) Maṅ. id.; ri γzár-gyi (ri gzar gyi) ṅos (ngos) steep declivity, cliff Thgy.; ri-γzar-čú (ri gzar chu) waterfall, cataract Glr.; γzar-kyóm-pa (gzar skyom pa) to get dizzy on a steep height Sch. — 2. vb. v. ₒdzár-ba ('dzar ba).

to be about, to be on the point, to prepare, mčóṅs-par (mchongs par), bsád-par (bsad par) γzás-pa-las (gzas pa las) when he was on the point of leaping, of killing Dzl.; rkó-bar (rko bar) γzás-so (gzas so) he prepared, began, to dig out.

1. shine, brightness, clearness, splendour; *táṅ-zi* (*thang zi*) W. looming, mirage. — 2. n. of a half-precious stone, variously coloured, brown, gray, streaked Glr, Pth. — 3. v. sub γzír-ba (gzir ba). — 4. v. bzi (bzi). — Comp. γzí-čan (gzi can) shining, bright, e. g. a star W. — γzi-brjíd (gzi brjid) 1. brightness, beauty, a fair, healthy complexion, = mdaṅs (mdangs), or joined with it, frq ; majesty, e. g. of deities etc. Dzl. 2. honour, esteem, celebrity; γzi-brjíd-čan (gzi brjid can) 1. bright, beautiful, majestic. 2. celebrated, famous, distinguished. — γzi-mdáṅs (gzi mdangs) 1. healthy appearance S.g. 2. vulgo also evening-red, evening-sky, nif. — γzi-byíṅ (gzi bying) = γzi-brjíd (gzi brjid) 1; γzi-byíṅ (gzi bying) nyáms-pa (nyams pa) looking poor, emaciated, worn out, from hunger, sufferings Stg.; γzi-byíṅ-čan (gzi bying can) bright, shining; γzi-ₒód (gzi 'od) bright gloss or lustre Lex.
col. for γzér-bu (gzer bu) a little nail W.

, resp. for mtóṅ-ba (mthong ba) and ltá-ba (lta ba) 1. to see, ₒóṅs-par ('ongs par) seeing that he had come Dzl.; in indirect questions, to see whether? — what sort of? — etc.; to see through, to get an insight Tar. 94, 6, Schf.; to look, śár-la (shar la) towards the east Glr.; to look (for), γzígs-pas (gzigs pas) mi ₒdug (mi 'dug) when he looked (for it), there was nothing to be seen; to look at, to regard, mind, esteem, sku-tsé-la (sku tshe la) mi γzígs-pa (mi gzigs pa) not regarding your Honour's life Dzl. — 2. equivalent to: to give, grant, sá-bon (sa bon) žig (zhig) tugs-rje (thugs rje) γzígs (gzigs) dgos (dgos) have the goodness to give me some seed, prob. only breviloquence for sá-bon (sa bon) žig (zhig) γnáṅ-bar (gnang bar) tugs-brtsé-bar (thugs brtse bar) γzígs (gzigs) śig (shig). — γzígs-rtén (gzigs rten) resp. present, gift, γzígs-rtén-du (gzigs rten du) skúr-ba (skur ba) to charge a person with the delivery of a present Pth. — *zig-dod-čan* (*zig dod can*) W. vain. — *zig-po* (*zig po*) W. neat, well dressed, resp. for mčór-po (mchor po). — γzígs-mo (gzigs mo) resp. for ltád-mo (ltad mo), mé-tog (me tog) dé-la (de la) γzígs-mor (gzigs mor) byón-pa-las (byon pa las) as he came in order to look at the flower Pth.
vessel, ship, float, ferry, also fig.; gru-γzíṅs (gru gzings) id., frq.; γzíṅs (gzings) čén-po (chen po) žig (zhig) byás-te (byas te) equipping a large vessel Glr.;
